meaning: adv.整洁地,优美的,巧妙的,[酒类]纯的
examples: Her hair was neatly combed back.
He folded his paper neatly and sipped his coffee.
She summarized her plan very neatly.
Her hanging clothes were organized by color and type, her shoes neatly ordered on the shelves.
Her narrative fits rather neatly with concerns at the UK level, particularly among UK Conservatives.
